what: The three datasets at a glance
title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: olympic_athletes headline long-format table. medal_table editions companion tables make per-team-event medal counting edition-level lookups easy without re-aggregating 300k+ rows.

title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: quick sanity check — participations per Games, season:
code: data(olympic_athletes) str(olympic_athletes, give.attr = FALSE, vec.len = 2) #> 'data.frame':    315090 obs. of  15 variables: #>  $ ID    : int  12068 35094 35698 38123 41160 ... #>  $ Name  : chr  "Arthur Charles Blake" "Angelos Fetsis" ... #>  $ Sex   : chr  "M" "M" ... #>  $ Age   : int  24 NA 22 23 21 ... #>  $ Height: num  NA NA NA 154 NA ... #>  $ Weight: num  NA NA NA 45 NA ... #>  $ Team  : chr  "United States" "Greece" ... #>  $ NOC   : chr  "USA" "GRE" ... #>  $ Games : chr  "1896 Summer" "1896 Summer" ... #>  $ Year  : int  1896 1896 1896 1896 1896 ... #>  $ Season: chr  "Summer" "Summer" ... #>  $ City  : chr  "Athina" "Athina" ... #>  $ Sport : chr  "Athletics" "Athletics" ... #>  $ Event : chr  "Athletics Men's 1,500 metres" "Athletics Men's 1,500 metres" ... #>  $ Medal : chr  "Silver" NA ... tab <- table(olympic_athletes$Year, olympic_athletes$Season) tail(tab, 10) #>        #>        Summer Winter #>   2008  13602      0 #>   2010      0   4402 #>   2012  12920      0 #>   2014      0   4891 #>   2016  13688      0 #>   2018      0   5119 #>   2020  14536      0 #>   2022      0   5247 #>   2024  13659      0 #>   2026      0   5413

title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: Medals olympic_athletes per-player: every member gold-winning ice hockey roster Medal = "Gold" row. matches rgriff23 schema right shape athlete-level analysis, inflates team counts sum naively. headline medal-table totals, use medal_table instead.
code: # Every roster member of the men's ice hockey gold medal team # at Beijing 2022 gets a Gold row: hockey_2022 <- subset(   olympic_athletes,   Year == 2022 &     Event == "Ice Hockey Men's Ice Hockey" &     Medal == "Gold" ) nrow(hockey_2022) #> [1] 0

title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: medal_table covers five new editions (2018-2026) uses IOC per-team-event convention. Top five NOCs Paris 2024 gold count:
code: data(medal_table) head(subset(medal_table, year == 2024), 5) #>           games year season noc                    country gold silver bronze #> 153 2024 Summer 2024 Summer USA              United States   40     44     42 #> 154 2024 Summer 2024 Summer CHN People's Republic of China   40     27     24 #> 155 2024 Summer 2024 Summer JPN                      Japan   20     12     13 #> 156 2024 Summer 2024 Summer AUS                  Australia   18     19     16 #> 157 2024 Summer 2024 Summer FRA                     France   16     26     22 #>     total #> 153   126 #> 154    91 #> 155    45 #> 156    53 #> 157    64 paris <- subset(medal_table, year == 2024) paris <- paris[order(-paris$gold, -paris$total), ] head(paris[, c("noc", "country", "gold", "silver", "bronze", "total")], 5) #>     noc                    country gold silver bronze total #> 153 USA              United States   40     44     42   126 #> 154 CHN People's Republic of China   40     27     24    91 #> 155 JPN                      Japan   20     12     13    45 #> 156 AUS                  Australia   18     19     16    53 #> 157 FRA                     France   16     26     22    64

what: editions: metadata for the new five Games
title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: Use enrich olympic_athletes edition-level facts (host country, opening date, total medal events) without scraping anything .
code: data(editions) editions[, c("games", "city", "country",              "opening_ceremony", "closing_ceremony",              "participants", "medal_events")] #>         games           city                    country opening_ceremony #> 1 2018 Winter    PyeongChang          Republic of Korea       2018-02-09 #> 2 2020 Summer          Tokyo                      Japan       2021-07-23 #> 3 2022 Winter        Beijing People's Republic of China       2022-02-04 #> 4 2024 Summer          Paris                     France       2024-07-26 #> 5 2026 Winter Milano-Cortina                      Italy       2026-02-06 #>   closing_ceremony participants medal_events #> 1       2018-02-25         2793          102 #> 2       2021-08-08        11319          339 #> 3       2022-02-20         2786          109 #> 4       2024-08-11        10763          329 #> 5       2026-02-22         2932          111

title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: 1896-2016 rows come rgriff23/Olympic_history, originally scraped sports-reference.com. 2018-2026 rows scraped olympedia.org using pipeline ismayc/olympic-moderndive-data. build script data-raw/DATASET.R reproduces every .rda data/ CSVs data-raw/. See ?olympic_athletes, ?medal_table, ?editions full column-level documentation.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Chester Ismay. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ismay C (2026). olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/moderndive/olympicAthletes.
code: @Manual{,   title = {olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026},   author = {Chester Ismay},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/moderndive/olympicAthletes}, }

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
text: tidy R data package covering every athlete-event participation modern Olympic Games, Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026 — 315,000 rows.

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
text: schema olympic_athletes matches original rgriff23/Olympic_history dataset, existing notebooks analyses keep working.
code: library(olympicAthletes)  data(olympic_athletes)   # ~315,000 athlete-event rows, 1896-2026 data(medal_table)        # verified medal table for 2018-2026 data(editions)           # metadata for the five new editions  ?olympic_athletes

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
text: Rows 1896-2016 come rgriff23 dataset (originally scraped sports-reference.com). Rows 2018-2026 scraped olympedia.org using pipeline olympic-moderndive-data repository. See data-raw/DATASET.R reproducible build.

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
text: Medal counts per-player, per-team-event. team gold (e.g. Ice Hockey Men) produces one Medal = "Gold" row per roster member. Group Medal NOC divide team-event medals team size reproduce IOC medal table — use medal_table companion dataset uses IOC convention. Bio coverage (Height/Weight) ranges ~80% older Winter Games ~25% Paris 2024 / Milano-Cortina 2026. Olympedia bios newer less-prominent athletes sparse — pattern original 1896-2016 source.

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
text: CC 4.0. Please cite package, rgriff23 source, Olympedia.

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
code: Ismay, C. (2026). olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026. R package version 0.1.0.  Griffin, R. (2018). Olympic history: longitudinal data scraped from www.sports-reference.com. https://github.com/rgriff23/Olympic_history  OlyMADMen. Olympedia. https://www.olympedia.org/

what: Edition-level metadata for the five new Games (2018-2026) — editions
title: Edition-level metadata for the five new Games (2018-2026) — editions
text: One row per Olympic edition added extend original dataset, verified totals participants, NOCs, medal events, disciplines, plus opening/closing ceremony dates.

title: Edition-level metadata for the five new Games (2018-2026) — editions
Edition-level metadata for the five new Games (2018-2026) — editions
text: data frame 5 rows 14 variables: edition_id Integer. Olympedia's internal edition number     (60, 61, 62, 63, 72). games Character. e.g. "2024 Summer". year Integer. season Character. city Character. Host city. country Character. opening_ceremony, closing_ceremony Date. ISO-8601. participants Integer. Olympedia's headline participant count. nocs Integer. Number NOCs represented. medal_events Integer. Number medal events. disciplines Integer. Number distinct disciplines. notes Character. Free-text edition notes. source Character. Olympedia URL.

title: Edition-level metadata for the five new Games (2018-2026) — editions
text: https://www.olympedia.org/

title: Edition-level metadata for the five new Games (2018-2026) — editions
code: data(editions) editions[, c("games", "city", "participants", "medal_events")] #>         games           city participants medal_events #> 1 2018 Winter    PyeongChang         2793          102 #> 2 2020 Summer          Tokyo        11319          339 #> 3 2022 Winter        Beijing         2786          109 #> 4 2024 Summer          Paris        10763          329 #> 5 2026 Winter Milano-Cortina         2932          111

what: Verified medal tables for the 2018-2026 editions — medal_table
title: Verified medal tables for the 2018-2026 editions — medal_table
text: One row per (Games, NOC) verified Gold/Silver/Bronze counts cross- checked olympedia.org Wikipedia/IOC. Use rather aggregating olympic_athletes want per-team-event medal convention (one medal per gold-winning ice hockey team, 25).

title: Verified medal tables for the 2018-2026 editions — medal_table
Verified medal tables for the 2018-2026 editions — medal_table
text: data frame 273 rows 9 variables: games Character. e.g. "2026 Winter". year Integer. season Character. "Summer" "Winter". noc Character. Three-letter IOC code. country Character. Full country/team name. gold, silver, bronze Integer. Medal counts. total Integer. Sum three.

title: Verified medal tables for the 2018-2026 editions — medal_table
text: https://www.olympedia.org/ (manually verified per edition).

title: Verified medal tables for the 2018-2026 editions — medal_table
code: data(medal_table) subset(medal_table, year == 2024)[1:10, ] #>           games year season noc                    country gold silver bronze #> 153 2024 Summer 2024 Summer USA              United States   40     44     42 #> 154 2024 Summer 2024 Summer CHN People's Republic of China   40     27     24 #> 155 2024 Summer 2024 Summer JPN                      Japan   20     12     13 #> 156 2024 Summer 2024 Summer AUS                  Australia   18     19     16 #> 157 2024 Summer 2024 Summer FRA                     France   16     26     22 #> 158 2024 Summer 2024 Summer NED                Netherlands   15      7     12 #> 159 2024 Summer 2024 Summer GBR              Great Britain   14     22     29 #> 160 2024 Summer 2024 Summer KOR          Republic of Korea   13      9     10 #> 161 2024 Summer 2024 Summer ITA                      Italy   12     13     15 #> 162 2024 Summer 2024 Summer GER                    Germany   12     13      8 #>     total #> 153   126 #> 154    91 #> 155    45 #> 156    53 #> 157    64 #> 158    34 #> 159    65 #> 160    32 #> 161    40 #> 162    33

what: olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026 — olympicAthletes-package
title: olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026 — olympicAthletes-package
text: tidy, long-format dataset every athlete-event participation modern Olympic Games. Extends rgriff23 Olympic_history dataset (1896-2016) five additional editions scraped olympedia.org: PyeongChang 2018, Tokyo 2020, Beijing 2022, Paris 2024, Milano-Cortina 2026.

title: olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026 — olympicAthletes-package
text: olympic_athletes: ~315,000 athlete-event rows. medal_table: verified medal table 2018-2026. editions: edition-level metadata new five.

title: olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026 — olympicAthletes-package
text: Maintainer: Chester Ismay chester.ismay@gmail.com

title: Olympic athlete-event participations, 1896-2026 — olympic_athletes
text: One row per (athlete, Games, event) participation across every modern Summer Winter Olympic Games Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026. Rows 1896-2016 come rgriff23 Olympic_history dataset (originally scraped sports-reference.com); rows 2018-2026 scraped olympedia.org.

title: Olympic athlete-event participations, 1896-2026 — olympic_athletes
text: data frame 314,749 rows 15 variables: ID Integer. Unique athlete ID. Original IDs 1..135571;     athletes new 2018-2026 editions get IDs starting     1,000,000 avoid collision. Name Character. ASCII-folded athlete name. Sex Character. "M" "F". Age Integer NA. Approximate age start     Games, computed date birth (Feb 1 reference Winter,     Jul 15 Summer). Height Numeric NA. Height centimetres. Weight Numeric NA. Weight kilograms. Team Character. Country/team name displayed source. NOC Character. Three-letter IOC code (e.g. "USA",     "", "ROC"). Games Character. "<year> <Summer/Winter>", e.g.     "2026 Winter". Year Integer. Calendar year Games. Tokyo 2020     coded 2020 even though held 2021 (IOC convention). Season Character. "Summer" "Winter". City Character. Host city. Sport Character. Sport name. 2018-2026 rows normalised     original sport names possible (e.g. "Cycling Track"     "Cycling Road" collapse "Cycling"). Event Character. Event name including sport prefix. Medal Character NA. "Gold", "Silver",     "Bronze", NA. Per-player: every member     medal-winning team roster gets row team's medal.

title: Olympic athlete-event participations, 1896-2026 — olympic_athletes
text: 1896-2016: Griffin, R. (2018). Olympic_history.     https://github.com/rgriff23/Olympic_history 2018-2026: https://www.olympedia.org/ (scraped April 2026).

title: Olympic athlete-event participations, 1896-2026 — olympic_athletes
code: data(olympic_athletes) head(olympic_athletes) #>      ID                       Name Sex Age Height Weight          Team NOC #> 1 12068       Arthur Charles Blake   M  24     NA     NA United States USA #> 2 35094             Angelos Fetsis   M  NA     NA     NA        Greece GRE #> 3 35698 Edwin Harold "Teddy" Flack   M  22     NA     NA     Australia AUS #> 4 38123                 Carl Galle   M  23    154     45       Germany GER #> 5 41160       Dimitrios P. Golemis   M  21     NA     NA        Greece GRE #> 6 57441  Konstantinos Karakatsanis   M  NA     NA     NA        Greece GRE #>         Games Year Season   City     Sport                        Event  Medal #> 1 1896 Summer 1896 Summer Athina Athletics Athletics Men's 1,500 metres Silver #> 2 1896 Summer 1896 Summer Athina Athletics Athletics Men's 1,500 metres   <NA> #> 3 1896 Summer 1896 Summer Athina Athletics Athletics Men's 1,500 metres   Gold #> 4 1896 Summer 1896 Summer Athina Athletics Athletics Men's 1,500 metres   <NA> #> 5 1896 Summer 1896 Summer Athina Athletics Athletics Men's 1,500 metres   <NA> #> 6 1896 Summer 1896 Summer Athina Athletics Athletics Men's 1,500 metres   <NA> table(olympic_athletes$Year, olympic_athletes$Season) #>        #>        Summer Winter #>   1896    380      0 #>   1900   1936      0 #>   1904   1301      0 #>   1906   1733      0 #>   1908   3101      0 #>   1912   4040      0 #>   1920   4292      0 #>   1924   5233    460 #>   1928   4992    582 #>   1932   2969    352 #>   1936   6506    895 #>   1948   6405   1075 #>   1952   8270   1088 #>   1956   5127   1307 #>   1960   8119   1116 #>   1964   7702   1778 #>   1968   8588   1891 #>   1972  10304   1655 #>   1976   8641   1861 #>   1980   7191   1746 #>   1984   9454   2134 #>   1988  12037   2639 #>   1992  12977   3436 #>   1994      0   3160 #>   1996  13780      0 #>   1998      0   3605 #>   2000  13821      0 #>   2002      0   4109 #>   2004  13443      0 #>   2006      0   4382 #>   2008  13602      0 #>   2010      0   4402 #>   2012  12920      0 #>   2014      0   4891 #>   2016  13688      0 #>   2018      0   5119 #>   2020  14536      0 #>   2022      0   5247 #>   2024  13659      0 #>   2026      0   5413  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Top medal-winning NOCs at Paris 2024 (one row per athlete-medal)   library(dplyr)   olympic_athletes |>     filter(Year == 2024, !is.na(Medal)) |>     count(NOC, Medal) |>     tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Medal, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>     arrange(desc(Gold + Silver + Bronze)) } # }

title: olympicAthletes 0.1.0
text: Initial release. olympic_athletes — 314,749 athlete-event rows spanning Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026, extending rgriff23 Olympic_history dataset (1896-2016) five additional editions scraped olympedia.org. medal_table — 273-row verified medal table five new editions (2018, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2026), one row per (Games, NOC). editions — 5-row metadata table covering opening/closing dates, participants, NOC count, medal events, disciplines new edition.
